title: Log signing chain integrity — cryptographic signature drift on evidence archive
exclusions: Does NOT validate the authenticity of the originating source system; only the unbroken signature chain once evidence reaches the archive.
evidence: Saved search 'log_signature_chain_verification' (hourly) outputs signature-chain breaks with block-number and expected-vs-actual hash to the compliance summary index.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Detects breaks in the integrity component of the trail; confidentiality and availability components need other controls.
Integrity controls for ePHI are directly implemented by an unbroken hash-chain on the evidence archive.
PCI 10.3.4 'file integrity monitoring or change-detection mechanisms on audit logs' is satisfied by a cryptographic-signature chain that emits failures.
A broken signature chain is a textbook ITGC deficiency but the control also needs management attestation.
Derived from GDPR@2016/679 Art.32(1)(b) (parent assurance='partial'). UK GDPR preserves parent clause numbering; inherited assurance degraded one step.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A deliberate rewrite of an archive block (test fixture) causes a hash mismatch versus the parent block's digest, and the UC fires within the next hourly interval naming the specific block and hash pair.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Normal append-only archive writes with correctly linked hashes do NOT fire.

dataSources: Evidence archive signature-verification events (e.g., sourcetype=archive:signer emitted by the signer service), WORM repository state REST API, KMS signing audit logs.
spl:
index=compliance sourcetype=archive:signer earliest=-1h
| eval block_parent_hash=coalesce(parent_hash,"GENESIS")
| stats values(block_hash) as block_hashes latest(expected_parent_hash) as expected_parent by block_id archive_id
| where expected_parent!=block_parent_hash AND block_parent_hash!="GENESIS"
| table archive_id block_id block_parent_hash expected_parent block_hashes
description: Continuously verifies that every new block in the write-once evidence archive references the immediately-preceding block by cryptographic hash. A mismatch is definitive evidence of either a faulty archive writer or a deliberate rewrite — either way a finding that regulators treat as a break in the chain of custody.
value: Replaces the quarterly 'we sampled 10 logs and checked hashes' audit procedure with a continuous control whose output is either 'zero breaks for the period' or a named remediation record. This is the evidence regulators actually ask for.
implementation: (1) Ensure the archive signer emits a per-block event with block_id, block_hash, parent_hash, and expected_parent_hash; (2) schedule the UC hourly; (3) route hits to compliance_summary; (4) SOAR auto-opens a P1 with the relevant archive owner; (5) run an automated rebuild drill monthly to ensure verification code itself is not silently broken.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Evidence archive signature-verification events (e.g., sourcetype=archive:signer emitted by the signer service), WORM repository state REST API, KMS signing audit logs..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ensure the archive signer emits a per-block event with block_id, block_hash, parent_hash, and expected_parent_hash; (2) schedule the UC hourly; (3) route hits to compliance_summary; (4) SOAR auto-opens a P1 with the relevant archive owner; (5) run an automated rebuild drill monthly to ensure verification code itself is not silently broken.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=compliance sourcetype=archive:signer earliest=-1h
| eval block_parent_hash=coalesce(parent_hash,"GENESIS")
| stats values(block_hash) as block_hashes latest(expected_parent_hash) as expected_parent by block_id archive_id
| where expected_parent!=block_parent_hash AND block_parent_hash!="GENESIS"
| table archive_id block_id block_parent_hash expected_parent block_hashes
```

Understanding this SPL

**Log signing chain integrity — cryptographic signature drift on evidence archive** — Replaces the quarterly 'we sampled 10 logs and checked hashes' audit procedure with a continuous control whose output is either 'zero breaks for the period' or a named remediation record. This is the evidence regulators actually ask for.

Documented **Data sources**: Evidence archive signature-verification events (e.g., sourcetype=archive:signer emitted by the signer service), WORM repository state REST API, KMS signing audit logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: compliance; **sourcetype**: archive:signer. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=compliance, sourcetype=archive:signer, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **block_parent_hash** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by block_id archive_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Filters the current rows with `where expected_parent!=block_parent_hash AND block_parent_hash!="GENESIS"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Log signing chain integrity — cryptographic signature drift on evidence archive**): table archive_id block_id block_parent_hash expected_parent block_hashes


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline of verified vs broken blocks, scatter of block_id × hash-mismatch severity, single-value '% of archive blocks verified (30d)'.
visualization: Timeline of verified vs broken blocks, scatter of block_id × hash-mismatch severity, single-value '% of archive blocks verified (30d)'.
knownFalsePositives: Archive signer upgrades that rotate the hash function produce a one-off legitimate 'parent-missing' at the rotation boundary — record the rotation in archive_rotations.csv and allow-list by timestamp.
